statement: 1D bin packing: given a list of item sizes `items` (floats in (0, 1]) and bin capacity 1.0, return a packing as a list of bins, where each bin is a list of item indices. Every index must appear exactly once and no bin may exceed capacity 1.0. Fewer bins = better. Design the best heuristic you can (e.g. improve on first-fit-decreasing); you have up to 20 seconds of compute.
